title: Clidemia alata
taxonomy: Clidemia alata is classified under the family Melastomataceae. It belongs to the order Myrtales and the clade Rosidae. The species is further classified as a member of the genus Clidemia, which is native to the Americas. The classification of Clidemia alata reflects its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants. This classification is essential for understanding the plant's ecological roles and potential uses.

Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and benefits of Clidemia alata.

intro: Clidemia alata, also known as winged clidemia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its distinctive winged seeds and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Clidemia alata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Clidemia alata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a syrup or tincture, to harness its health benefits.
